one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding from Indigenous Services Canada including Indigenous Community Support Fund
economic_notes: COVID-19 pandemic caused facility closures and temporary employee terminations since April 2020. Nation has investments in Pimee Well Servicing Ltd (17%), Pimee Limited Partnership, Seven Lakes Oilfield Services Limited Partnership (8.33%), and 1678902 Alberta Ltd (100%). Oil and gas royalties and surface rights payments administered under Indian Oil and Gas Act. Nation received $2,654,769 in additional COVID-19 funding from ISC.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 not 2024. Consolidated entity includes Beaver Lake Education Authority - Amisk Community School, Beaver Lake Wah-Pow Treatment Centre, Beaver Lake Health Services, and Beaver Lake Government and Industry Relations. GBEs accounted for by equity/modified equity method include Pimee Well Servicing Ltd, Pimee Limited Partnership, and Seven Lakes Oilfield Services Limited Partnership. Significant COVID-19 impacts including facility closures and additional ISC funding of $2,654,769. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($10,928,874) + ISC Health Canada ($3,206,490) + CMHC ($123,818) + HRD Canada ($225,786) = $14,484,968. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. Infrastructure expenses represent Public Works segment. Band Government used for administration expenses. Large increase in deferred revenue from $4.9M to $9.4M driven by ISC Technical Services deferred $4,018,630.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
